method_atom: First-passage / survival analysis of a continuous stochastic diffusion process governed by the diffusion (Fokker-Planck) equation confined by a time-dependent absorbing boundary that recedes at a constant rate, yielding a closed form for the joint probability density of surviving up to a given time while occupying a given position, conditioned on the starting position.
object_atom: A Brownian particle with diffusion constant D moving inside an expanding d-dimensional sphere of initial radius L_0 whose surface, growing at constant rate c, acts as an absorbing boundary.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is survival probability of a diffusion under a linearly moving absorbing boundary; what is domain-specific is the physical Brownian particle inside a geometrically expanding d-dimensional sphere.
